headlines.text: Trump signals willingness to end Iran war even if Strait of Hormuz stays closed, stocks surge
US gas prices hit $4 a gallon for first time since 2022 as Iran war squeezes global oil supply
Israel and US launch new wave of strikes on Isfahan; Hegseth refuses to rule out ground invasion
Iran strikes Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai; parliament votes to toll the Strait of Hormuz
China and Pakistan release five-point peace plan calling for immediate ceasefire and open Strait
Supreme Court sides with Christian therapist challenging Colorado conversion therapy ban
DHS shutdown drags to record 44-plus days as Senate negotiations stall yet again
Project Hail Mary crosses $300M globally in second weekend, tops 2026 box office
Dow rallies 533 points on Iran peace report; S&P 500 posts best day in weeks to close volatile March
Nvidia takes $2 billion stake in Marvell; Warren Buffett tells Becky Quick he sold Apple too soon

breaking.text: BREAKING: Hegseth at Pentagon briefing refuses to rule out US ground troops entering Iran

stories.hed: Trump hints at ending war; Hegseth says boots on ground still on the table
stories.dek: The Wall Street Journal reports Trump told aides he is willing to wind down hostilities even with the Strait of Hormuz largely still closed — which sent stocks soaring and oil wavering, because nothing calms markets like ambiguity. Meanwhile Hegseth held his first Pentagon briefing in nearly two weeks and confirmed that, yes, ground troops remain an option, in case Iran needed one more thing to worry about.

stories.hed: China and Pakistan unveil five-point ceasefire plan as Iran parliament votes to toll the Strait
stories.dek: Pakistani FM Ishaq Dar flew to Beijing and met with Wang Yi, and together they called for an immediate ceasefire, peace talks, and restoration of Strait of Hormuz passage. Iran's parliament, presumably not reading the same memo, voted to institute a toll on ships transiting the Strait — so peace may cost you.

stories.hed: Supreme Court backs conversion therapy ban challenge, deals blow to LGBTQ+ protections
stories.dek: The Supreme Court ruled Tuesday in favor of a Christian therapist who argued Colorado's ban on conversion therapy for minors violated her First Amendment rights. The decision sets precedent that therapist-patient conversations are constitutionally protected speech, with implications well beyond Colorado.

stories.hed: DHS shutdown hits record 44-plus days with no deal in sight as TSA lines stretch to infinity
stories.dek: Senate negotiations over Homeland Security funding remain deadlocked, with the partial government shutdown now the longest in US history. TSA wait times have hit record highs at major airports during spring break travel, which is a great time to rediscover your love of driving — except gas is now $4 a gallon.

stories.hed: Trump tells European allies to go to the Strait of Hormuz and 'just TAKE IT' — diplomacy is doing great
stories.dek: With fuel shortages squeezing allies, Trump posted on Truth Social urging countries that need jet fuel to 'build up some delayed courage, go to the Strait, and just TAKE IT,' adding the US 'won't be there to help you anymore.' Spain responded by banning US and Israeli forces from using its military bases, which is one way to RSVP.

markets.context: Stocks rallied sharply after the Wall Street Journal reported Trump told aides he is willing to end the Iran war even if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed, giving Wall Street its best day in weeks to close out a brutal March.

gas.note: Congratulations, your commute now costs more than your lunch — and possibly your lunch.

popCulture.hed: Project Hail Mary tops $300M globally in week two, cementing Ryan Gosling's biggest solo hit ever
popCulture.dek: Ryan Gosling's sci-fi adaptation of Andy Weir's novel crossed $300 million worldwide in its second weekend with a remarkable 32% drop — besting Oppenheimer's second-weekend hold. The film, directed by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller, carries a 95% on Rotten Tomatoes and an A CinemaScore, making it the rare movie that critics, audiences, and your dad all agree on.

genZ.definition: A deeply unsettling meme character born from the Italian brainrot craze on TikTok — an animated Indonesian wooden plank that interrupts whatever you are doing with a repetitive, chaotic drumming sound. It has exploded across FYPs in March 2026 as a filter and sound drop used to represent any chaotic intrusion into normal life.
genZ.usage: Me trying to track the Iran war, gas prices, the DHS shutdown, AND my 401k simultaneously: *Tung Tung Tung Sahur enters the chat*

tldr: The US-Iran war entered its 32nd day with Trump floating an exit even as Hegseth refused to rule out a ground invasion, leaving the world in the diplomatic equivalent of a choose-your-own-adventure with live ordnance. Gas hit $4 a gallon for the first time since 2022, stocks rallied on peace rumors, and Ryan Gosling saved the planet on screen while the rest of us just tried to afford the drive to the theater.
